rag_rules: Place AGREE annex blocks in component implementation scope when binding to subcomponent ports or implementation-specific logic.
Ensure contracts in component type scope reference only visible features, not subcomponent paths.
generic_rules: Preserve AADL scope boundaries: type-level annexes must use only externally visible features; implementation-level annexes can bind internal connections.
When propagating state across components, use implementation annexes to map subcomponent outputs to parent outputs via assignments.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use 'assign output = expression;' in implementation annexes to bind internal signals to visible outputs.
AGREE scalar types are int, real, bool — never use integer, boolean, or other variants.
anti_patterns: Do not reference subcomponent ports (e.g., subcomp.port) in component type annexes — they are not visible at that scope.
Do not place implementation-specific bindings or equations in component type annexes — move them to the implementation scope.
